title: coelogyne lepantoensis
intro: Coelogyne lepantoensis, also known as the Lepanto Coelogyne, is a species of orchid native to the Philippines. It is a type of flowering plant used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Philippines, due to its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate various health issues such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The herbal preparations of Coelogyne lepantoensis typically involve infusions or decoctions of the plant's leaves and roots, which can be consumed as a tea or used topically for wound healing and skin care.
